title: Asymptotics for Hecke eigenvalues with improved error term
speaker: Jasmin Matz
abstract: Asymptotics for the distribution of Hecke eigenvalues in families of automorphic forms are useful to study families of L-functions, provided one has a sufficiently good estimate for the error term of the asymptotic. I want to present ongoing joint work with T. Finis in which we give an upper bound for the error term with an explicit dependence on the Hecke operator. For certain applications an improvement of this bound would be necessary, but to go beyond our bound it seems necessary to remove part of the geometric side of the trace formula.

proof_stages.label: Automorphic family and Weyl-law normalization
proof_stages.summary: The speaker fixes a split semisimple group over Q, the spherical unramified automorphic spectrum, the infinitesimal-character ball F_t, and the Weyl-law main term Lambda(t) used for normalization.

proof_stages.label: Hecke-average formulation of Satake equidistribution
proof_stages.summary: The counting problem is refined from infinitesimal characters to finite-place Hecke eigenvalues by averaging tr pi(tau) over pi in F_t, normalized by Lambda(t), so that the limiting statement becomes Satake-parameter equidistribution.

proof_stages.label: Uniform error goal and admissible groups
proof_stages.summary: After recalling the earlier GL(n) bound with opaque dependence on the Hecke operator, the speaker states the ongoing goal: under standard restrictions on intertwining normalizations and L-functions, prove an error of order roughly ||tau||_1 T^{-1/2+epsilon} with constants independent of tau.

proof_stages.label: Paley-Wiener localizing test functions
proof_stages.summary: The trace formula is fed with a product test function whose finite part is the Hecke operator tau and whose archimedean spherical part is built via Paley-Wiener theory to localize the spectral parameter near mu with support independent of mu.

proof_stages.label: Spectral extraction and central geometric main term
proof_stages.summary: Integrating the trace formula over the spectral ball produces the desired cuspidal Hecke average on the spectral side, while the central terms on the geometric side give the main term Lambda(t) times the sum of tau over the center.

proof_stages.label: Truncated-integral replacement for the noncentral geometry
proof_stages.summary: Instead of the fine geometric expansion, the remaining noncentral geometric contribution is controlled by Arthur-style truncated integrals, viewed as polynomials in the truncation parameter and transferred back to the trace formula by interpolation.

proof_stages.label: Archimedean majorant from spherical-function bounds
proof_stages.summary: A uniform estimate for elementary spherical functions near singular lambda and near K is used to dominate the archimedean test function by a compactly supported positive radial majorant with a gain of size roughly (1+||mu||)^{-1/2}.

proof_stages.label: Bruhat, lattice, and intertwining estimates completing the saving
proof_stages.summary: The truncated sum is decomposed by Bruhat cells, lattice-point estimates trade sums for derivatives, and intertwining-operator bounds reduce the final integral to one with a^{-1/2+epsilon} decay, yielding the desired mu-saving while preserving only the finite-place L1 norm and identifying obstacles to improving it.
